topic.what: Logic-based explainable AI framework for 5G intrusion detection combining Transformer IDS with rule extraction and LLM explanations
topic.how: Trains Transformer IDS, applies Integrated Gradients for feature attribution, extracts rules via surrogate decision tree, generates natural language explanations via LLMs with novel three-part validation
topic.why_matters: Addresses trust gap in security-critical domains by proving high performance (99.9% accuracy) and full interpretability can coexist without sacrificing transparency for marginal metric gains

characteristics.what_it_measures: Overall classification correctness on test set
characteristics.context: 9-class 5G IoT dataset with severe class imbalance
Macro average includes rare attack classes with minimal support

characteristics.what_it_measures: Agreement between extracted rules and Transformer predictions
characteristics.context: 16 rules extracted from depth-4 surrogate decision tree
Covers 99.998% of test cases after pruning lowest-support rule

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time per flow classification on CPU
Alignment of LLM explanations with feature attribution signs

good_for: explainable intrusion detection in IoT networks
requires: LLM API access for explanation generation

novelty: First XAI framework integrating Transformer IDS with logic rule extraction and validated LLM explanations
Novel three-part validation scheme for LLM-generated explanations (semantic similarity, attribution faithfulness, actionability)
Demonstrates performance-interpretability tradeoff can be resolved in security-critical domains
First expert study comparing human ratings with LLM-as-judge metrics for security explanations
open_problems: Cross-dataset validation across different 5G vendors and IoT deployment scenarios
Enhancing surrogate model fidelity to close remaining opacity gap
Investigating adversarial attacks targeting explanation-aware evasions
Automated rule-to-response pipelines for security orchestration
